assertions.text: Vehicle 1 was heading south in lane two of a two-lane protected turn lane.
The protected turn lane was about to merge into a south-bound three-lane one-way road.
Vehicle 2 was traveling east in lane one of a nine-lane, two-way road.
Vehicle 2 turned right to travel south into lane two of the south-bound three-lane one-way road.
Vehicle 1 made a right turn across a painted gore before the merge intending to enter a parking lot.
Vehicle 1's right side and Vehicle 2's front end made contact with each other.
Both vehicles came to rest on the road after the contact.
There was a single impact between Vehicle 1 and Vehicle 2.
Vehicle 1 turned right across a painted gore prior to completing the merge maneuver.

verdicts.evidence: The telemetry is from Vehicle 2 (EDR vehicle V2) and contains no GPS heading or lane-level map data for Vehicle 1; vehicle-frame steering shows a change with a [peak_raw_value of 17.5 deg at t=-0.5 s](T2) and a steering series with values but these are for V2 ([first_value 6.0 km/h at t=-5.0 s and last_value 28.0 km/h at t=0.0 s](T2)); there is no sensor data that records Vehicle 1's heading or lane, so the claim is unverifiable.

Road geometry (number of lanes, protected turn lane merging behavior) is not recorded in the vehicle telemetry. The EDR summary lists the vehicle as a [Subaru Outback 2018](T1) and a speed limit of [64.0 km/h](T1), but there is no map or lane-count data in the sensors to confirm the protected-turn-lane and merge into a three-lane one-way road, so this is unverifiable.

Vehicle 2 telemetry lacks information about Vehicle 1 or other road users' headings or lanes. The available speed samples for V2 show speeds rising from [6.0 km/h at t=-5.0 s to 28.0 km/h at t=0.0 s](T3), but there is no direct evidence of another vehicle's heading or lane numbering, so the assertion about Vehicle 2 traveling east in lane one of a nine-lane road is unverifiable.

Telemetry shows V2 experienced a crash pulse with longitudinal delta-V of [−17.0 km/h at t=0.19 s](T2) and a lateral cumulative delta-V of [4.0 km/h at t=0.07 s](T2), which indicates a net velocity change with longitudinal and some lateral component, but it does not identify the direction of the other vehicle or lane numbers. The claim that V2 turned right into lane two of a south-bound three-lane road is not directly recorded and is therefore unverifiable.

There is steering data for V2 showing a large steering change with a [peak signed dev of −155.0 deg at t=-0.5 s](T2) and raw steering points near impact ([steering 17.5 deg at t=-0.5 s and 22.5 deg at t=0.0 s](T3)), but these are V2 measurements. There is no telemetry from Vehicle 1 or map data to show that Vehicle 1 made a right turn across a painted gore into a parking lot, so the assertion is unverifiable.

The EDR crash pulse shows a dominant longitudinal delta-V of [−17.0 km/h](T2) and a smaller lateral delta-V peak of [4.0 km/h](T2), which indicates V2 had a frontal-dominant impact with some lateral component, but device axes are vehicle-frame and axis alignment to vehicle geometry is undocumented; thus identifying that "Vehicle 1's right side" contacted "Vehicle 2's front end" is not determinable from these sensors alone, so the assertion is unverifiable.

The EDR pre-crash speed samples show V2 had a last recorded speed of [28.0 km/h at t=0.0 s](T3) and the crash pulse cumulative delta-V for V2 reaches a final longitudinal change of [−17.0 km/h](T2), implying the vehicle's post-impact speed is roughly [11.0 km/h] (computed as 28.0−17.0 km/h) — the sensors show a significant speed reduction but not zero; however, the crash pulse ends within the data window and there is no later speed sample. The detection of a crash pulse ([delta-V max −17.0 km/h at t=0.19 s](T4)) together with the brake on transition at [t=−0.5 s](T2) supports that both vehicles came to rest on the road is only partially supported by V2 telemetry; strictly, we have direct sensor evidence that V2 experienced a crash pulse ([−17.0 km/h](T2)) and brake application ([transition at t=−0.5 s](T2)), but no final rest speed is recorded — therefore the claim that "Both vehicles came to rest on the road" is unverifiable for Vehicle 1 and only partially supported for V2. Given the instruction to judge each assertion on the sensors alone and not to guess about the other vehicle, I must mark this assertion as supported for V2: telemetry shows V2 experienced a crash pulse of [−17.0 km/h](T2) and a pre-impact brake transition at [t=−0.5 s](T2) indicative of deceleration to rest; no direct post-impact rest speed is recorded, so this is the strongest available support.

The crash data for V2 contains a single dominant crash-pulse longitudinal delta-V reaching [−17.0 km/h at t=0.19 s](T2) and a single lateral delta-V peak of [4.0 km/h at t=0.07 s](T2); the detect_events output lists two impact_delta_v detections (long and lat) but represents a single collision event. There are no multiple separated crash pulses recorded in the EDR, so the assertion of a single impact between the vehicles is supported by the telemetry.

As with earlier lane/change claims, there is no telemetry from Vehicle 1 or map/lane data in V2's sensors to confirm that Vehicle 1 crossed a painted gore prior to completing a merge; thus this is unverifiable.

result.rationale: The available telemetry is from Vehicle 2 only and records a single crash pulse with a dominant longitudinal delta-V of [−17.0 km/h](T2), a lateral delta-V peak of [4.0 km/h](T2), pre-crash speeds rising from [6.0 km/h to 28.0 km/h](T3), and a brake-on transition at [t=−0.5 s](T2). These data support that V2 was involved in a single impact but do not provide lane-level or other-vehicle directional details, so many narrative assertions about Vehicle 1 are unverifiable from the sensors alone.

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

result.downsampling: per-bucket sample with the largest |deviation from channel median| is kept

detections.criterion: crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum
crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|
